finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_16.md:23-25 — Two new paragraphs inserted into the Caleb audit section. Line 23 renders Nora encountering the sexual messages ('She had told Caleb what she wanted done to her, had used words she would have cut from a manuscript for being too exposed') and the specific horror that Simone's system generated responses calibrated by years of intimate friendship ('Nora had spent years telling her best friend how she liked to be touched and talked to and wanted... and Simone had taken all of it and entered it into a system and the system had whispered it back'). Line 25 renders the intimate photo ('the black bra, her face cropped out of frame because the bravery was about her body') and Simone's possible real-time access to it as engagement data. Somatic detail: gooseflesh traveling from wrists to shoulders (line 25), pulse in ears and hands not steady (line 25), body 'open in a way she'd forgotten it could be' (line 23). The passage renders the specific mechanism of violation — not abstract exposure but Simone evaluating Nora's sexual vulnerability as a metric — and does not retreat into summary.
finding_results.notes: Inserted between the October literary-reference audit and the financial audit, chronologically consistent with the chapter 10 timeline (January). The passage addresses both the sexual exchanges and the intimate photo as required by the acceptance test.

finding_results.evidence: The Simone text audit section (chapters/chapter_16.md:51-63) now uses at least 5 structurally distinct approaches: (1) Standard template at line 51 (Oct 16: quote → surface reading → subsurface reinterpretation). (2) Compressed rapid-scroll at line 53 (three texts quoted in sequence without individual re-readings, Nora speed-reading for pattern not surprise). (3) Three-read ambiguity at line 55 (Nov 28: read once as friend, again as scammer, third time as irreducibly both). (4) Stalled reinterpretation at line 57 (Jan 14: Nora's hands stop, she remembers Simone's trembling fingers, cannot locate the seam — 'maybe a person could engineer your suffering and then genuinely ache when they watched you feel it... there was no word for the thing Simone had done'). (5) Physical interruption at line 61 (fingers pressed against closed eyes, radiator clicking, siren Doppler-shifting) followed by immediate recognition at line 63 (scroll-forward with no re-reading needed — 'There was no second reading. It was a con artist's closing line'). The Jan 14 message at line 57 resists clean surface/subsurface parsing — Nora cannot resolve whether the holding was real, performed, or both, and the passage refuses to resolve it for her.
finding_results.notes: The compressed section (line 53) replaced two individually quoted messages with a rapid-fire accumulation. The physical interruption (line 61) breaks the scrolling rhythm with sensory detail (radiator, siren, formless light behind closed eyes). The immediate-recognition ending (line 63) inverts the audit's established pattern — no reinterpretation, just raw fact.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_16.md:83 — 'The name was a knuckle pressed into a bruise.' The knuckle/bruise image is preserved. The 'felt X arrive in her body like Y' template has been eliminated — the line now uses a direct metaphor ('was') instead of the arrival frame.
finding_results.notes: Chose Option B from the rewrite direction: replaced the frame while keeping the image. The direct construction ('was a knuckle') is stronger for this moment — Margo has just said Caleb's name, and the impact should be immediate, not described as arriving.

summary: All three findings addressed. CH16-CRAFT-01 (HIGH): Added two paragraphs to the Caleb audit rendering the sexual messages and intimate photo with full specificity and somatic detail — gooseflesh, pulse, the body's recognition of violation. CH16-CRAFT-02 (MEDIUM): Restructured the Simone text audit from a repetitive quote-surface-subsurface template into five formally distinct approaches including compressed rapid-scroll, irreducible ambiguity, physical interruption, and immediate recognition. CH16-MED-13 (MEDIUM): Replaced the 'arrive in her body like' formula with a direct metaphor while preserving the knuckle/bruise image. No new continuity contradictions introduced; all dates, amounts, and character details verified against the continuity sheet.
